constraints: No live opencode.ai or api.z.ai calls
Only local/static checks and existing local Go tests used

assertions.reason: config.example.yaml contains commented OpenCode Zen and separate OpenCode Go openai-compatibility examples. The Go entry uses name opencode-go, base-url https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1, api-key-entries with <OPENCODE_API_KEY>, prefix go, alias go-default, and parsed successfully along with the Zen entry.

README English docs describe Zen and Go as separate openai-compatibility providers sharing OpenCode API-key bearer auth through api-key-entries, explicitly not built-in OpenCode login/auth-file providers, with distinct Go routing via opencode-go/prefix go/go-default.

README states the documented OpenCode path sends OpenAI-compatible bearer-token requests to /chat/completions and explicitly excludes OAuth, /responses, WebSocket, and Anthropic /messages routes for these examples. Config comments also label OpenCode Go as OpenAI-compatible chat completions only.

Searches under cmd, internal, and sdk found only pre-existing generic comments mentioning OpenCode-style clients or issue references, with no opencode-go, opencode.ai endpoint, or first-class provider/runtime/auth/translator/registry/sdk special-case additions. git diff --name-only was empty before writing this report.

Validation used only local file inspection, YAML parsing, git grep, and targeted Go tests. The executable/config search found no opencode.ai references outside static docs/config examples, and no live calls or real credentials were used or created.

Docs and config use distinct routing names/prefixes/aliases: opencode-zen with prefix zen and alias zen-default, versus opencode-go with prefix go and alias go-default, preventing Go and Zen alias collisions.

commandsRun.observation: Whole file parsed as YAML; extracted OpenCode Zen/Go commented examples parsed with expected names, prefixes, base URLs, placeholders, and aliases.
No changed files were present before report/evidence creation.

commandsRun.observation: Only generic/pre-existing comment references were found; no opencode-go provider implementation or endpoint special cases.
No executable test/script/config references to opencode.ai outside config.example.yaml static example.

commandsRun.observation: README and config snippets document OpenCode Go/Zen as OpenAI-compatible chat completions only, with explicit exclusions for OAuth, /responses, WebSocket, and /messages.
